GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Celebrate 50 years of the Superstar sneaker with adidas Originals", "Five decades of the brand's most recognisable shoe.", "The adidas Superstar sneaker is as iconic as they come. Worn by trailblazing change makers and boundary-pushing individuals across the globe, the sneaker is no doubt the most recognisable of any adidas shoe–and for good reason. Now celebrating 50 years since its inception, the adidas Superstar is ringing in its landmark anniversary with the launch of Change Is a Team Sport a series of initiatives that serve to showcase the noteworthy shoe's impact on culture as a whole. In celebration of the instantly-recognisable shoe and its long-standing impact, Superbad's Jonah Hill directed a film that stars the likes of Ninja, Kerwin Frost, Blackpink, Pharrell Williams, Yara Shahidi, Mariah Duran and Anitta, which works to highlight the idea that change is, in fact, a team sport. "There's no knowledge without the older generation who did it first. And there's no change or progression without the new generation," says Hill of the campaign, which officially launched during the 62nd annual Grammy Awards, hosted at the Staples Center in Los Angeles on January 26, 2020. "So if those two generations come together, I feel like that creates the atomic charge for great art and great creativity," Hill adds of the exciting new film, which sees American skateboarder Jenn Soto explore a facility filled with adidas' family of creators working towards change. This new film marks the first of many initiatives that adidas Originals will unveil this year, which will each serve to showcase the power of teamwork and celebrate five decades of the adidas Superstar. If you've ever worn the adidas Superstar sneaker, then you know the shoe's biggest drawcard is its endless potential for individualisation. With its iconic modern design, it is no wonder that over the years the sneaker has become the go-to for artists, designers, musicians, and athletes alike. Originally worn by NBA players such as the Los Angeles Lakers' Kareem Abdul-Jabbar after the style changed the design of basketball shoes forever, it was soon adopted by New York hip hop crews, and later, the skate world who embraced the shoes largely for their wearability and their reputation for being outstandingly durable. "They looked great," explains the Superstar's original designer, Chris Severn, "but they also helped the athlete perform better". Now, with its leather upper and rubber shell-toe offering, the adidas Superstar sneaker's iconic silhouette has moved from the world of sport to the streets, and in turn, infiltrating the world of fashion with their wear by street-style stars around the world."
